calendar.matters:
PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE CALENDAR FOR ATTORNEY COOPER BRINSON
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Inactivate Attorney

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY COOPER BRINSON
Off Calendar Oct-10-2019 Continued To Nov-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs, Notice Of Motion, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Apr-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Douglas A. Crowder, Esq., Johsnon & Associates, 213-621-3000, appearing for Plaintiff; (CourtCall)
James R. Wheaton, Esq., First Amendment Project, 510-208-7744, appearing for Defendant(s)John/Jane Doe; (CourtCall)
Cooper Brinson, Esq., Civil Liberties Defense Center, 541-687-9180, appearing for Defendant(s) Does 1-10; (CourtCall)

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

OTHERs JOHN DOE and JANE DOE's Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs,

Movant John/Jane Doe's motion for attorneys' fees and costs pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1987.2 is denied. That statute provides in pertinent part, "in making an order pursuant to motion made . . . under section 1987.1, the court may in its discretion award the amount of the reasonable expenses incurred in making or opposing the motion, including reasonable attorney's fees, if the court finds the motion was made or opposed in bad faith or without substantial justification or that one or more of the requirements of the subpoena was oppressive." (Code Civ. Proc. 1987.2(a).) In contrast to this discretionary provision, section 1987.2(d) makes such an award of attorney's fees mandatory in connection with motions to quash certain subpoenas for personally identifying information for use in an action pending outside California if the underlying action "arises from the moving party's exercise of free speech rights on the Internet and the respondent has failed to make a prima facie showing of a cause of action"; in such cases, "the court shall award the amount of the reasonable expenses incurred in making the motion, including reasonable attorney's fees." (Id. 1987.2(d) [emphasis added].)

When Doe Defendants originally moved to quash the subpoena duces tecum, they also moved for monetary sanctions. As the plain statutory language quoted above makes clear, the trial court had discretion in ruling on that motion to award fees and costs to Doe Defendants. In granting the motion to quash, however, the judge pro tem (to whom both parties stipulated) did not grant Doe Defendants' request for sanctions. Under the circumstances, the judge pro tem must be deemed to have exercised his discretion to deny Doe Defendants' request. Doe Defendants' renewed request to the Court is inconsistent with section 1987.2, which provides that a court may award fees "in making an order" on a motion to quash a subpoena. Further, it does not comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, which provides that a party filing a renewed application for an order a court has previously refused must submit an affidavit showing what "new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed" to justify the renewed application, and must show diligence with a satisfactory explanation for not presenting the new or different information earlier. (Code Civ. Proc. 1008(b), (e); see Evan Zohar Construction & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 833.) Finally, this Court lacks the authority to vacate an order made (or, as in this case, refused) by another judicial bench officer in the exercise of his or her discretion. (See People v. Saez (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1177, 1184-85 ["'For one superior court judge, no matter how well intended, even if correct as a matter of law, to nullify a duly made, erroneous ruling of another superior court judge [improperly] places the second judge in the role of a one-judge appellate court,' and thus 'an order "made in one department during the progress of a cause can neither be ignored nor overlooked in another department . . . ."'"].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
